steps: Suppose for contradiction there exists a homeomorphism $f: S^1 \to S^2$. Then $f_*: H_n(S^1) \xrightarrow{\cong} H_n(S^2)$ for all $n$.
But $H_1(S^1) \cong \mathbb{Z}$ while $H_1(S^2) = 0$. These groups are not isomorphic.
Intuitively: any loop in $S^2$ can be contracted to a point (since $S^2$ is simply connected), but the fundamental loop in $S^1$ cannot be contracted.
